title: Analysis of Head and Torso movements for Authentication
venue: IEEE 4th International Conference on Identity, Security, and Behavior Analysis (ISBA)

biometric.body_location: head, front trouser pocket, back trouser pocket

method.input_representation: 96 features per device (including Mean, Std Dev, Band Power, Energy, Median Frequency, IQR, Range, SNR, Spectral Entropy, DTW, Mutual Information, Correlation)
method.training_strategy: one session for training and one session for testing

evaluation.context: Achieved using GG with SF combination using LDA classifier in 'Activities with no Stairs' scenario

evaluation_protocol: Cross-session testing where training and testing were conducted in separate sessions spread across 28 days.

reported_limitations: GG gets overheated for higher sensor delay times

key_contribution: Demonstrated that head and torso movements are weakly correlated and can be combined to improve continuous authentication on Google Glass
main_strength: Evaluation includes cross-session testing over a 28-day period
main_weakness: No active spoofing or presentation attacks tested
insights.notes_for_review: The paper claims the low correlation between modalities prevents spoofing/replay, but this is a theoretical claim and not experimentally validated through an attack.
